title: Overlap & Gibson ligation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"30bj4","text":"This protocol is used to lagate two pieces of DNA together without digesting the fragment with restriction endonucleases.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3sqkj","text":"Set up a small box with ice, put DNA and enzymes on it.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5o69v","text":"Prepare the water bath to 37℃ to have Gibson assembly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"dur8r","text":"Please wear gloves for the experiment,  don't try to touch the lid after PCR program initiation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"ekkd5","text":"DNA fragments","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6keg6","text":"Primers","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hmvt","text":"ClonExpress II One Step Cloning Kit (Vazyme)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2rv6n","text":"2×High Fidelity Master Mix (MCLAB)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3vno7","text":"Nanodrop","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2o5f8","text":"Thermocycler","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4l6u5","text":"Water bath","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9u7mo","text":"DdH2O","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bg03f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Ligation of two DNA fragments by using cases below.
Select an appropriate cloning site on the vector that will be linearized.
Vector linearization: the linearized vector can be obtained by digesting the circular vector with restriction enzymes or by reverse PCR.
Amplify the insert DNA fragments with homologous sequences (for homologous recombination) of vector-upstream or -downstream by PCR using high fidelity DNA polymerase.
Detect DNA concentration of linearized vectors and inserts by Nanodrop.

Calculation of the amount of vectors: The optimal amount of vector for the recombination with ClonExpress II is 0.03 pmol, while the optimal amount of insert is 0.06 pmol (molar ratio of vector to insertion is 1:2), as roughly calculated as follows: The optimal mass of vector = [0.02 × number of base pairs] ng (0.03 pmol) The optimal mass of insert = [0.04 × number of base pairs] ng (0.06 pmol)
Dilute linearized vectors and inserts before recombination to make sure the loading accuracy. The volume of each component loaded should be no less than 1 μl.
Set up the following reaction on ice: A B 1 Linearized Vectors X μl 2 Inserts Y μl 3 5 × CE II Buffer 4 μl 4 Exnase II 2 μl 5 ddH2O Add to 20 μl
Incubate at 37℃ for 30 min and immediately place the tube at 4℃ or on ice.
Select an appropriate cloning site on the vector that will be linearized.
Vector linearization: the linearized vector can be obtained by digesting the circular vector with restriction enzymes or by reverse PCR.
Amplify the insert DNA fragments with homologous sequences (for homologous recombination) of vector-upstream or -downstream by PCR using high fidelity DNA polymerase.
Detect DNA concentration of linearized vectors and inserts by Nanodrop.
Calculation of the amount of vectors: Molar ratio of vector to insertion is 1:1

Set up the following reaction on ice (50μl): A B 1 Forward Primer (10 μM) 1μl 2 Reverse Primer (10 μM) 1μl 3 Fragment1(vector) X 4 Fragment2(insertion) Y 5 2×High Fidelity Master Mix (MCLAB) 25μl 6 ddH2O Add to 50μl The primer is used to amplify recombinant DNA fragment/circular DNA.
Program the thermocycler as follows: A B 1 Temperature Time 2 95/98℃ 5 min 3 95/98℃ 30 s 4 Tm-3~5℃ 30 s 5 72℃ 1kb/min 6 72℃ 5~10 min 7 16℃ ∞ Repeat 30 times in 3-5 steps
Use the palm centrifuge to mix the solution in PCR tube.
Put the PCR tube into the thermocycler and Run the program.
Using agarose gel electrophoresis to confirm if correct construct was present.
Use the palm centrifuge to mix the solution in PCR tube.
creator: Shuning Guo
